segments.s027-g33: به ایوان او is a fronted prepositional/locative phrase ('to/into his iwan') with او as the third-person possessive modifying ایوان. آتش is the direct object placed before the verb, and اندر is a locative particle reinforcing 'into/within'. فگند is an archaic poetic past-tense form (cf. فکند) meaning 'threw' or 'cast' and appears in final position as the main verb.
segments.s027-g34: ز پای is an ablative phrase 'from the foot/base (of)', where پای means the base/foundation. اندر here is adverbial/locative used with the verb, and آورد (past of آوردن) is a transitive past-tense verb meaning 'brought' or 'caused to come/ fall' (i.e., 'brought down'). کاخ بلند is the object phrase 'the lofty/tall palace', with the adjective بلند following the noun as is normal in Persian.
segments.s027-g17: فرانک بدو گفت is a short reported-speech introducer: فرانک = 'a Frank/European' (here an indefinite ethnonym, not a personal name), بدو = 'to him', and گفت is the past-tense verb 'said'. کای پاکدین begins the direct address: کای is the archaic vocative particle 'O', and پاکدین is a vocative noun 'pious one/holy of faith'.
segments.s027-g18: منم سوگواری ز ایران‌زمین = 'I am a mourner because of/for the land of Iran.' منم is the emphatic copular 'I am', سوگواری is a noun 'mourning/mourner', ز is the poetic preposition 'from/because of', and ایران‌زمین names the realm 'land of Iran.'
segments.s027-g19: بدان کاین is the archaic clause introducer 'بدان' = imper. 'know' (constructed here from ب + دان) followed by the conjunction کـ + این = 'that this/that', so the phrase introduces a content clause 'know that this precious child of mine...'. گرانمایه فرزند من = 'this precious/esteemed son of mine.'
segments.s027-g20: همی بود خواهد سر انجمن is an archaic periphrastic construction expressing future-in-the-past or destined outcome: همی is an emphatic/continuous particle, بود functions as a past auxiliary, and خواهد is the future/auxiliary 'will' — together rendering 'would be / was destined to be.' سر انجمن = 'the head/chief of the assembly.'
segments.s027-g21: تو را بود باید نگهبان او is a modal-experiencer construction: تو را (lit. 'to you' with را marking the affected person) + بود ('was') + باید ('must/ought') gives 'it was necessary for you' or 'you ought to be' followed by نگهبان او 'his guard/protector.'
segments.s027-g22: پدروار لرزنده بر جان او qualifies the manner of guarding: پدروار 'fatherly, like a father', لرزنده 'trembling/quivering' (a poetic adjective), بر جان او 'over his life' = 'for his life' (literally 'upon his soul'), so the whole phrase means 'trembling like a father for his life.'
segments.s027-g23: پذیرفت فرزند او نیکمرد = 'he accepted / he received his son as a nīk-mard (a good/upright man)'. In context پذیرفت is transitive 'accepted / took (to be)', followed by the theme فرزند او 'his son' and the predicate noun نیکمرد 'a good man / hero.'
segments.s027-g24: نیاورد هرگز بدو باد سرد is a negative wish/blessing in which نیاورد (literally 'bring not') with هرگز 'never' and باد سرد 'cold wind' functions idiomatically: باد here is the optative/benedictive particle ('may'), so the line means 'may a cold wind never come upon him' (i.e., 'may he never suffer cold/calamity').
segments.s027-g9: ببرم is the first‑person singular present/subjunctive (optative/determinative) of بردن meaning “I will take/carry.” It precedes the following locative phrase پی از خاک جادوستان, where پی means “after/in pursuit of” or here adverbially “away/from,” and از خاک جادوستان is a simple prepositional phrase “from the land/soil of the magicians.” Word order is V + adverbial/prepositional phrase.
segments.s027-g10: شوم is the first‑person subjunctive of شدن used optatively/intentionaly as “may I be / I will be / I will go (arrive).” تا here means “as far as / up to,” and سر مرز is a fixed locution literally “head/top of the border” (= the frontier). هندوستان is the destination “India.” Together the clause expresses reaching the Indian frontier.
segments.s027-g11: شوم + ناپدید is a subjunctive construction meaning “may I become vanished / may I disappear.” از میان گروه is the prepositional phrase “from among the company/group,” with از میان marking separation out of a crowd. The verb + participial/adjectival complement pattern is idiomatic.
segments.s027-g12: برم is the first‑person form of بردن “to take/carry” (here “I will take”). خوب‌رخ is a noun/adjective meaning “fair‑faced / beautiful one”; را is the definite object marker attaching to that object. به + مکان (البرز کوه) gives the goal “to Mount Alborz,” with the noun order البرز کوه = ‘Alborz mountain.’
segments.s027-g13: بیاورد is a past/epic narrative form meaning “he brought” or “he produced.” فرزند را is the definite object marked by را. چون + نوند is a simile/comparison: چون = “like/as,” and نوند here refers to the musical mode/name (Navand) or a poetic epithet, rendering the child as ‘Navand‑like.’
segments.s027-g14: چو/چو (چو here = چون) starts a simile “as/like,” so چو مرغان بر آن تیغ کوه بلند = “like birds upon that point/edge of the high mountain.” بر آن تیغ کوه بلند is a locative phrase: بر = on/upon, آن تیغ کوه = that mountain’s point/crest, بلند = high/lofty. The image compares the child’s bearing to birds on the ridge.
segments.s027-g15: یکی here means “a certain” (introducing an indefinite person). مرد دینی = “a pious/religious man.” بر آن کوه بود is the locative+copula: بر = on/upon, آن کوه = that mountain, بود = was (past copula). Word order is determiner + noun + adjective then location + past copula.
segments.s027-g16: که introduces a relative clause “who/that.” از کار گیتی is a genitive construct meaning “from/of the affairs of the world” (i.e. worldly affairs). بی‌اندوه بود is the predicate “was without sorrow / was free of grief,” so the whole relative clause qualifies the man as unconcerned with worldly cares.
segments.s027-g25: خبر شد is an impersonal/inchoative construction: شد (past of شدن) with خبر as the theme means 'it became known' / 'news arrived'. به ضحاک marks the indirect object/recipient ('to Zahhak'). بدروزگار is a postpositive epithet modifying ضحاک ('Zahhak the ill-fated').
segments.s027-g26: از آن introduces the content of the news: از here means 'concerning/about' (concerning that). گاو برمایه is a compound phrase: گاو 'cow' modified by برمایه 'source/resource (of wealth)'. و links the two items, and مرغزار names the other item 'meadow/pasture'.
segments.s027-g27: بیامد is past tense 'came' / 'arose'. از آن کینه: از آن marks source ('from that') and کینه is the noun 'hatred' (the thing that arose). چون پیل مست is a simile 'like a drunken elephant' (چون = 'like/as', followed by noun + adjective).
segments.s027-g28: مر is an archaic/dative first‑person form ('to me' / 'for me') placed before the object phrase; آن گاو برمایه را is the definite direct object (را marks the object) 'that wealth-bearing cow'; کرد پست is a verb + predicate adjective construction where کرد (past of کردن) means 'made/caused' and پست is the resultative adjective 'low/demeaned' — literally 'made that cow low (for me)'.
segments.s027-g29: همه هر چه is an emphatic double-quantifier meaning 'everything/whatever'; دید is the past verb 'he saw' functioning as a relative clause 'whatever he saw'. اندر او means 'in/into it' (او refers back to the place mentioned earlier), and چارپای ('quadruped') is the class of animals being referred to — placed before the verb in the classical word order, with the verb following in the next segment.
segments.s027-g30: بیفگند is the past of a verb meaning 'threw/cast' or 'drove into' — here 'he cast/drove (them) into it'. و coordinates the two actions. زیشان is z- (from/of) + ایشان ('them') meaning 'from them' or 'of them' as source, and بپرداخت جای is a verbal phrase meaning 'deprived (them) of their place' / 'took away their place' (an archaic sense here).
segments.s027-g31: سبک here functions adverbially 'lightly/with light step' (i.e., swiftly). سوی is the preposition 'toward', خان is the title 'Khan' and فریدون the proper name. شتافت is the past tense 'he hastened/ran' (past of شتابیدن). Word order is adverb + prepositional phrase + verb.
segments.s027-g32: فراوان is an adverbial intensifier 'abundantly/thoroughly', placed before the verb پژوهید 'he searched/investigated'. و links the clauses; کس را نیافت is a negative result clause 'he found no one' — کس here, in the context of the negation نیافت, means 'no one', را is the object marker, and نیافت is the past negative 'did not find'.
segments.s027-g1: This is a negative past-state construction: نشد is the past negative of شدن ('did not become' / 'was not'). سیر ('satiety') is the predicate, while ضحاک is the referent (name) placed after it by poetic inversion: 'Zahhak was not sated'. از آن جست‌جوی is a prepositional phrase 'from that search' (جست‌جوی = 'search'), with از آن ('from that') preceding the noun phrase.
segments.s027-g2: شد is the simple past/preterite of شدن used here as 'became' or 'arose'. از گاو گیتی gives the source/agental phrase 'from the world's cow' (گیتی = 'the world'). پر از ... شد is the usual pattern 'became full of ...' but inverted for poetic rhythm: گفت‌گوی ('conversation/talk') is the complement of پر از ('full of').
segments.s027-g3: دوان is an adverbial/participial form meaning 'running/rushing' modifying آمد ('came'); word order here is adverb — subject — verb (دوان مادر آمد). سوی + noun marks direction 'toward the meadow' (سوی مرغزار). This is a simple dynamic clause: 'the mother rushed to the meadow.'
segments.s027-g4: چنین ('thus') introduces reported speech; گفت is the past-tense verb 'said' (here used as 'spoke'), and با in older/poetic Persian can mark the addressee ('to' someone) as well as 'with' — here 'با مرد زنهاردار' = 'to the stern man'. زنهاردار follows مرد as an adjectival/morphological compound meaning 'stern/admonitory'.
segments.s027-g5: که introduces a subordinate content clause 'that ...'. اندیشه‌ای ('a thought') is indefinite (the -ای ending) and is the topic; در دلم is a locative phrase 'in my heart' where دلم = دل + -م (possessive '-my'). ایزدی is a postpositive adjective 'divine', describing the thought.
segments.s027-g6: فراز آمده‌ست is a perfective/result clause: فراز ('from on high' / 'above') + آمده‌ست ('has come') = 'has come from above'. از ره بخردی is a source/instrument phrase 'by/through the way of wisdom' (ره = way; بخردی = 'wisdom/prudence'), so the thought 'has come from on high by means of wisdom.'
segments.s027-g7: همی کرد باید is an older/poetic word order for 'باید همی کرد' — همی is an emphatic/continuous particle used with past-stem to express necessity or habitual action, کرد is 'do', and باید is the modal 'must/ought'. کزین ('from this') + چاره نیست ('there is no remedy/solution') is a fixed negative existential: 'there is no remedy from this.'
segments.s027-g8: که introduces the explanatory clause 'that ...'. فرزند و شیرین is a coordinated noun phrase 'the child and Shirin' (شیرین here read as a proper name). روانم is روان + -م ('my soul / my mind') placed after the nouns to mark whose soul is meant, and یکیست is an equative/identity predicate 'is one' — 'that my child and Shirin are one in my soul' or 'are one (in spirit).'

sentences.s027-s17: He hurled fire into his iwan and from its base brought down the lofty palace.
sentences.s027-s9: A Frank said to him, 'O pious one, I am a mourner for the land of Iran.'
sentences.s027-s10: Know that this precious son of mine would be made the chief of the assembly.
sentences.s027-s11: It was necessary that you be his guard, trembling like a father over his life.
sentences.s027-s12: His son accepted (was taken as) a good man; may cold wind never befall him.
sentences.s027-s5: I will take [her] out of the land of sorcerers and go as far as the frontier of India.
sentences.s027-s6: May I vanish from among the company and carry the fair‑faced one to Mount Alborz.
sentences.s027-s7: He brought the child — like Navand — like birds upon that sharp mountain crest.
sentences.s027-s8: There was a certain pious man on that mountain who was free from the cares of the world.
sentences.s027-s13: News of that wealth-bearing cow and the meadow reached Zahhak, the ill-fated.
sentences.s027-s14: From that sprang a hatred; like a drunken elephant he humbled that wealth-bearing cow.
sentences.s027-s15: He drove into it every quadruped he saw, and dispossessed them of their places.
sentences.s027-s16: Lightly he hurried to Khan Fereydun; he searched thoroughly and found no one.
sentences.s027-s1: Zahhak was not sated by that search; from the world's cow the world became full of talk.
sentences.s027-s2: The mother ran toward the meadow; thus she spoke to the stern man.
sentences.s027-s3: That a divine thought is in my heart — it has come from on high by the path of wisdom.
sentences.s027-s4: One must act, for there is no remedy for this; that my child and Shirin are one in spirit.
